title: crotalaria sessiliflora
intro: Crotalaria sessiliflora, also known as the buttonpod or button pod crotalaria, is a flowering plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain a compound called 3-hydroxy-4-pyrone, with reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Crotalaria sessiliflora has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the seeds are often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
